revision.text:
[[fr:Ame-no-Oshikumone]][[ja:天押雲根命]][[simple:Ame-no-Oshikumone]] {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name =Ame-no-Oshikumone | image =File:Kasuga-taisha, Wakamiya-jinja, honden.jpg | caption =Wakamiya Shrine, a subshrine of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=}} that worships {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|en|draft:Ame-no-Oshikumone|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}} | script_name = Japanese | script = | other_names = | cult_center ={{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=}}
{{ill|Omura shrine|ja|大村神社 (伊賀市)|every|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omura shrine|1=Omura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20043474}} | symbols = | consort = | parents =; {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}; {{ill|Q108039939|qid=Q108039939|1=Kushimachi no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Takuhata Chijihime no Mikoto|qid=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}}| siblings = | children = {{ill|Amenotaneko|ja|天種子命|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}} | texts = | mother = {{ill|Himegami|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}. | father = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}Shibata, Joue. Saitama Sōsho. (1929) Volume 1: Chichibu Shi, p 111, Chichibu hikomikoto o keizu 『埼玉叢書. 第1巻』内『秩父志』内111頁「秩父彦命御系図」. Sanmeisha Shibata, Joue. Saitama Sōsho. (1929) Volume 1: Chichibu Shi, p 111, Chichibu hikomikoto o keizu 『埼玉叢書. 第1巻』内『秩父志』内111頁「秩父彦命御系図」. Sanmeisha|equivalent1={{nihongo|{{ill|Manjushri|lt=Mañjuśrī|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjushri|qq=}}|文殊|Monju|qq=}}
{{nihongo|{{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokiteśvara|qq=}}|觀音|Kannon|qq=}}|equivalent1_type=Buddhist|member_of={{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|en|draft:Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}}| gender = {{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}} | offspring = {{ill|Amenotaneko|qid=Q106107027|1=Amenotaneko}}; {{ill|Q106107009|qid=Q106107009|1=Amanami no Mikoto}} | equivalent5_type = Japanese | equivalent5 = {{ill|Mañjuśrī|qid=Q471696|1=Manjushri}}; {{ill|Āryāvalokiteśvara|qid=Q124684411|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara}} }} {{complex|qq=}} {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|en|Draft:Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}} (天押雲根命) is a Japanese deified historical figure and the [[Oyagami|Oyagami]] of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi clan|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Ame no koyane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} and {{ill|Himegami|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}.{{Cite book|title=Japanese mandalas : representations of sacred geography|last=Ten Grotenhuis|first=Elizabeth|date=1999|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|isbn=0824820002|oclc=39181008|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Amenotaneko|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}.* {{Open access|qq=}} {{Cite book |author=太田亮 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130845/702?__lang=en |title=姓氏家系大辞典 |date=1934 |publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会 |editor1-last=上田 |editor1-first=萬年 |editor1-link=:ja:上田萬年 |volume=第1巻 |pages=1257–1261 |chapter=大中臣 オホナカトミ |oclc=673726070 |id={{JPNO|47004572|qq=}} |editor2-last=三上 |editor2-first=参次 監修 |editor2-link=:ja:三上参次 |authorlink=:ja:太田亮 |qq=}} * [[:ja:中村英重|中村英重]]「中臣氏と家門」（所収:[[:ja:吉村武彦|吉村武彦]] 編『律令制国家と古代社会の詳細』（[[:ja:塙書房|塙書房]]、[[:ja:2005th year |2005]]（[[:ja:平成|平成]]17th year ）） ISBN 978-4-8273-1196-9） and the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara clan|qq=}}.Shibata, Joue. Saitama Sōsho. (1929) Volume 1: Chichibu Shi, p 111, Chichibu hikomikoto o keizu 『埼玉叢書. 第1巻』内『秩父志』内111頁「秩父彦命御系図」. Sanmeisha{{Cite book|title=Japanese mandalas : representations of sacred geography|last=Ten Grotenhuis|first=Elizabeth|date=1999|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|isbn=0824820002|oclc=39181008|qq=}} He is in a famous {{ill|Painting|en|Painting|qid=Q11629|lt=painting|1=Painting}} "Mandala of Wakamiya of Kasuga Shrine".{{Cite web |title=Mandala of Wakamiya of Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga wakamiya mandara) {{!|qq=}} Japan {{!|qq=}} Nanbokuchō period (1336–92) |url=https://www.metmuseum.org/art/collection/search/39482 |access-date=2023-12-02 |website=The Metropolitan Museum of Art |language=en|qq=}} Now on display at the {{ill|Metropolitan Museum of Art|en|Metropolitan Museum of Art|qid=Q160236|lt=Metropolitan Museum of Art|1=Metropolitan Museum of Art}}. He was associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] and the {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|qid=Q582846|lt=Izumo Province|1=Izumo Province}}. He was identified as the bodhisattva {{nihongo|{{ill|Manjushri|lt=Mañjuśrī|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjushri|qq=}}|文殊|Monju|qq=}} or as {{nihongo|{{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Avalokiteśvara|qq=}}|觀音|Kannon|qq=}}.{{Cite journal|last=Giolai|first=Andrea|date=2020-09-22|title=Encounters with the Past: Fractals and Atmospheres at Kasuga Wakamiya Onmatsuri|url=https://brill.com/view/journals/jrj/9/1-3/article-p213_8.xml|journal=Journal of Religion in Japan|volume=9|issue=1-3|pages=213–247|doi=10.1163/22118349-00901002|issn=2211-8349|qq=}} He is a component of {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|en|draft:Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}}{{Cite book|title=The miracles of the Kasuga deity|last=Tyler|first=Royall|date=2016|publisher=Columbia University Press|isbn=9780231534765|oclc=954193203|qq=}}. He was added much later than the rest of them and is said to be the divine child of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Ame no koyane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} and {{ill|Himegami|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}. two other components of {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|en|draft:Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}}.{{Cite book|title=The protocol of the gods: a study of the Kasuga cult in Japanese history|author=Grapard, Allan G.|date=1992|publisher=University of California Press|isbn=0520070976|oclc=25873140|qq=}} The importance of the multifaceted kami was that it became a template for future worshipers who wanted to combine several deities to pray to at once.{{Cite book|title=Handbook of Japanese mythology|last=Ashkenazi|first=Michael|date=2011|publisher=ABC-CLIO|isbn=9781849728560|oclc=755870995|qq=}} His two parent kami {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Koyane|qq=}} and {{ill|Himegami|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}} were ''{{ill|bunrei|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bunrei|qq=}}'' transferred from {{ill|Hiraoka Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraoka Shrine|qq=}}, and the shrine was thus given the name of ''Moto-Kasuga'' ("former Kasuga").{{cite book|title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido|last1=Yoshiki|first1=Emi|date=2007|publisher=PHP Institute|isbn=978-4569669304|language=Japanese|qq=}}{{cite book|title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri|last1=Okada|first1=Shoji|date=2014|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=978-4582945614|language=Japanese|qq=}} Wakamiya Shrine, a subshrine of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=}} is dedicated to the fifth new member {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|en|Draft:Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}}.https://www.mlit.go.jp/tagengo-db/common/001559851.pdf{{Cite web|title=Wakamiya Shrine {{!|qq=}} Search Details|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/tagengo-db/en/H30-00882.html|access-date=2024-03-20|website=Japan Tourism Agency,Japan Tourism Agency|language=en|qq=}} == History == {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|en|Draft:Ame-no-Oshikumone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}} was a {{ill|History of Japan|en|History of Japan|qid=Q130436|lt=japanese historical|1=History of Japan}} figure. They lived in the early Japanese history and are related to the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=Japanese royal family|1=Imperial House of Japan}}. He is an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi clan|qq=}} and a child of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Ame no koyane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}. His son is {{ill|Amenotaneko|ja|天種子命|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}. === Genealogy === {{Chart/start|align=center|summary=An example family tree|qq=}} {{Chart| | | |ILL|-|v|-|Hime| |ILL={{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|en|Draft:Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}}|Hime={{ill|Himegami|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}. |qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL={{ill|Amenotaneko|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}|qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL={{ill|Usami no Mikoto|ja|宇佐臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usami no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL={{ill|Mishokutsuomi no Mikoto|ja|御食津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishokutsuomi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505720|1=Mishokutsuomi no Mikoto}}|qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL={{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}}|qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL={{ill|Nashitomi|ja|梨迹臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashitomi|qq=}}|qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL={{ill|Kamikikikatsu|ja|神聞勝命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikikikatsu|qq=}}|qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL={{ill|Kushiukameshi no Mikoto|ja|久志宇賀主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushiukameshi no Mikoto|qq=}}|qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL={{ill|Kuninazu no Ōkashima no Mikoto|lt=Kuninazu no Ōkashima no Mikoto|lt=Kuninazu no oukashima|ja|国摩大鹿島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninazu no oukashima|qq=}}|qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL={{ill|Omikiyama no Mikoto|ja|臣狭山命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omikiyama no Mikoto|qq=}}|qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL={{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|ja|中臣烏賊津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Ikatsu|qq=}}|qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL=O-o-obase-no-mikoto (大小橋命) |qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL=Nakatomi no Amahisa-no-kimi |qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL=Nakatomi no Abiko |qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL=Nakatomi no Mahito |qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL=Nakatomi no Kamako |qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL=Nakatomi no Kuroda |qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL=Nakatomi no Tokiwa |qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL=Nakatomi no Katanoko |qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL=Nakatomi no Mikeko |qq=}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| |qq=}} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL='''{{ill|Fujiwara no Kamatari|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Kamatari|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara clan|qq=}} progenitor)[[FRank Brinkley|Brinkley, FRank]] and [[:en:Dairoku Kikuchi|Dairoku Kikuchi]]. (1915). ''A History of the Japanese People from the Earliest Times to the End of the Meiji Era.'' New York: Encyclopædia Britannica. [https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/413099 OCLC 413099]|qq=}} {{Chart/end|qq=}} ==See Also== * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|en|draft:Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|en|Family tree of Japanese monarchs|qid=Q3249915|lt=Family tree of Japanese monarchs|1=Family tree of Japanese monarchs}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kasuga Faith|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|天押雲根命|version=104250819|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:User:Ame-no-Oshikumone]] }} [[simple:User:Ame-no-Oshikumone]] {{everybodywiki link|Ame-no-Oshikumone}} {{everybodywiki link|Ame-no-Oshikumone|Ame-no-Oshikumone}} [[fr:Ame-no-Oshikumone]] [[fr:User:Ame-no-Oshikumone]] [[ja:天押雲根命]] [[ja:Ame-no-Oshikumone]] {{wikidata link|Q91088537}} [[Category:Nakatomi clan]] [[Category:Amatsukami]] [[Category:Japanese deities]]
